source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, points A, B, and C are on the circle with center O. If $\angle C=20^\circ$ and $\angle B=30^\circ$, what is the measure of $\angle A$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Let $\because$ $\angle C=20^\circ$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle O=2\angle C=40^\circ$,\\
$\because$ $\angle O+\angle A=\angle B+\angle C$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle A=30^\circ+20^\circ-40^\circ=\boxed{10^\circ}$
extraction_notes: raw_answer_text extracted from the final boxed result in the solution field

image_dependency: question_text says "As shown in the figure," indicating the diagram is required
the needed geometric relations are not fully specified in text alone
multi_step: solution uses at least two reasoning steps: convert an inscribed angle to a central angle, then combine angle relations to solve for angle A
the problem combines multiple given conditions: angle C=20 degrees and angle B=30 degrees
geometry inference depends on reading the circle configuration from the image rather than direct single-step arithmetic
verifiability: target answer is a single numeric angle value, which is easy to check
answer_text provides an explicit derivation ending in 10 degrees
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present, so the core semantics appear recoverable
the problem is strongly image-dependent, and the diagram is referenced as necessary in the question
the only clear risk is the very small image resolution (84x93), which may limit reliable recovery of geometric details
under high-recall collection rules, this risk supports low_priority rather than reject
